evidence: File begins with '# Check PRP' — no YAML frontmatter block (--- delimiters absent); name and description fields missing
description: Command file lacks YAML frontmatter entirely; plugin system cannot register it
suggested_fix:
Add ---
name: check-prp
description: Validate a Product Requirements Proposal against project conventions
allowed-tools: [Read, Glob, Grep]
--- before the H1

evidence: tools: frontmatter declares 'BashOutput' which is not in the standard Claude Code tool catalog (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep, LS, WebFetch, WebSearch, Task, TodoWrite)
description: BashOutput is not a standard Claude Code tool; declaration will be ignored or cause errors at registration
suggested_fix: Replace 'BashOutput' with 'Bash' in the tools list

evidence: tools: frontmatter declares 'KillBash' which is not in the standard Claude Code tool catalog
description: KillBash is not a standard Claude Code tool; Bash tool covers process termination
suggested_fix: Remove 'KillBash' from the tools list

evidence: Frontmatter does not include a model: field

suggested_fix: Add 'model: claude-sonnet-4-6' to frontmatter

evidence: No '## Output Format' or equivalent section found; agent describes tool capabilities but not response structure
description: No output format section; response shape is undefined
suggested_fix: Add '## Output Format' specifying how findings are structured (e.g. per-tool sections, code examples, recommendation format)

evidence: No '## Output Format' or equivalent section found
description: No output format section; security findings have no declared structure (severity labels, finding schema)
suggested_fix: Add '## Output Format' with severity levels (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) and per-finding schema

evidence: '## Output Standards' section is a quality rubric (type safety criteria, naming conventions) not an output format specification
description: Output Standards is a quality checklist, not a response format spec; reader cannot determine response shape
suggested_fix: Add '## Output Format' specifying response length, code block conventions, and explanation structure

evidence: Output format deferred to rubric skill via 'Output in the priority order the rubric\'s Output Expectations specifies'; skill is loaded but format is not declared inline
description: Partial credit: output format is defined in the referenced skill, but the agent does not summarize the format for standalone readability
suggested_fix: Add a brief '## Output Format' that either summarizes or cites the specific section in the skill SKILL.md

evidence: Skill sets reasoning mode ('think harder') but contains no '## Output Format' or equivalent section
description: Mode-setting skill does not specify how its activation affects output structure
suggested_fix: Add brief '## Output Format' noting that this skill modifies reasoning depth, not response shape; outputs follow the invoking agent's format

evidence: No '## Output Format' or equivalent section found in document body
description: No output format section; response shape is undefined
suggested_fix: Add '## Output Format' section describing the structure of the agent's responses

evidence: Term 'proper' used as quantifier (occurrence 1); e.g. 'proper security controls' — no specific standard cited
description: Vague quantifier 'proper' without a referenced standard or concrete criterion
suggested_fix: Replace with specific standard: e.g. 'OWASP-compliant input validation'

evidence: Term 'proper' used as quantifier (occurrence 2)
description: Second occurrence of vague quantifier 'proper'
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criterion

evidence: 'Stage Appropriate Files' uses 'appropriate' as a quantifier without defining the selection criterion
description: Vague quantifier 'appropriate' leaves file-staging scope undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit rule: e.g. 'Stage files modified in this session that are not in .gitignore'

evidence: "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@perplexity-ai/mcp-server"] — no version pin; resolved at session start against npm registry
description: Unpinned npx MCP server; a compromised or yanked package version could run arbitrary code at session start
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: npx -y @perplexity-ai/mcp-server@<version>; review changelog before upgrading

evidence: "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "gemini-mcp-tool"] — no version pin
description: Unpinned npx MCP server for gemini-mcp-tool
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: npx -y gemini-mcp-tool@<version>

evidence: "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@chinchillaenterprises/mcp-grok"] — no version pin; third-party org with low npm visibility
description: Unpinned npx MCP server from a low-visibility npm org; heightened supply-chain risk
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version and audit org provenance before use

evidence: "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "omnifocus-mcp-enhanced"] — no version pin
description: Unpinned npx MCP server for omnifocus-mcp-enhanced
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version: npx -y omnifocus-mcp-enhanced@<version>

evidence: curl -s -L "$CLAUDE_DOCS_URL" — outbound HTTP to docs.claude.com; no fallback if network unavailable
description: Script makes outbound HTTP request; fails silently on network unavailability and exposes session to external URL changes
suggested_fix: Add error handling: check curl exit code and provide a graceful fallback message; document the external dependency in SKILL.md

evidence: gh search code — outbound GitHub API call; rate-limits apply; no auth check before invocation
description: Script calls GitHub API without verifying authentication; may silently fail or consume API rate limit
suggested_fix: Add 'gh auth status || exit 0' guard before the search call; document gh dependency in SKILL.md

evidence: All 6 command files (check-prp, check-spec, execute-prp, execute-spec, generate-prp, generate-spec) begin with H1 headings with no YAML frontmatter; plugin.json is correct; omission is systemic across the entire commands/ directory
description: Systemic frontmatter omission across all commands in the spec-driven plugin; the plugin.json describes the plugin correctly but no individual commands can be registered
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter to all 6 command files in a single PR; reuse the plugin.json description keywords for each command's description field

evidence: All 6 researcher agents (claude-researcher, codex-researcher, gemini-researcher, grok-researcher, perplexity-researcher, researcher) omit tools: from frontmatter; omission is consistent across all agents suggesting deliberate design rather than oversight
description: Researcher agents rely on Claude Code default tool grants rather than declaring an explicit tool scope; if defaults change the agents silently acquire or lose capabilities
suggested_fix: Advisory only: if researchers only need WebFetch and WebSearch, add 'tools: [WebFetch, WebSearch]' to each agent's frontmatter to make scope explicit

evidence: SKILL.md references LOGIC.md and UI.md as companion files; both files exist in the same directory but the skill does not declare how they are loaded or whether the skill loader co-loads sibling files automatically
description: Sibling file dependencies (LOGIC.md, UI.md) are referenced but not declared; behavior depends on undocumented skill-loader semantics
suggested_fix: Advisory only: add a note in SKILL.md explaining how companion files are loaded, or consolidate into a single SKILL.md
